summary_one_line: A dotted column reference (ID.ID, the '.' operator) in the SQL input is needed so resolveExprStep dispatches pExpr->op to the TK_DOT case arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The SQL input must contain a dotted column/table reference (an ID '.' ID token sequence such as M.L), so the parser emits a TK_DOT expression node; the switch in resolveExprStep then takes the TK_DOT case arm at line 98355 and descends into the pExpr->op!=TK_ID else-block (lines 98366-98386).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed begins 'SELECT M.L. .L.4' with literal '.' (0x2e) bytes separating identifier characters (M, L), forming the ID.ID dotted-reference structure that parses to a TK_DOT node; value_profile resolved 9/10 trials and was the only seed to take the true side (W=1 true at L98355).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds contain no dotted-reference structure: they are runs of '+' (0x2b), digits/commas ('SELECT ( 111111,'), or '~'/'<' fillers, so the parser never builds a TK_DOT node and the switch falls through to TK_ID / TK_VARIABLE / TK_LT arms instead; naive blocked 9/10 with 0 hits on the true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted token/character comparisons (e.g. the byte comparison that classifies an expression operator as the '.' / TK_DOT token) move closer to matching, preserving partial-progress seeds that naive's pure edge-coverage discards; this Hamming/prefix-distance bucketing lets value_profile incrementally assemble the rare ID.ID dotted syntax that naive cannot stumble onto with havoc bytes alone.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the TK_DOT case arm of the switch in resolveExprStep, and the true side is the globally blocked side.
Taking the TK_DOT case requires pExpr->op to not be TK_ID, descending into the dotted-reference else-block exercised only by the winner.
The value_profile winner seed contains a dotted identifier reference with literal '.' separators.
The naive loser seeds lack dotted-reference structure, being filled with '+' / digit / comma runs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 53 45 4c 45 43 54 2b 53 45 2b ac 53 ac ac ac ac   SELECT+SE+.S....

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile resolved the branch in 9/10 trials while naive blocked it in 9/10, the decisive divergence.
Only the winner ever drove the TK_DOT true side; the loser took it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L98355  T=1 F=9  T=0 F=593  case TK_DOT: {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's distinguishing technique is CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose compared operands move closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Neither involved fuzzer uses I2S substitution, so the divergence is not attributable to I2SRandReplace.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile distance-bucket feedback), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage dash-row check does not apply; the prompt's MECHANISM CONTEXT confirms value_profile uses no I2SRandReplace mutator, so no I2S-floor signal is expected or claimed.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on the TK_DOT '.' token where value_profile fails to resolve faster than naive, or where naive resolves at parity, would refute the CMP_MAP-gradient attribution for this dotted-reference feature.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is shown, so the inference that the '.' (TK_DOT) token specifically — rather than some other co-occurring structure in that single seed — is the CMP_MAP-rewarded feature rests on a small sample.
